_meta.title: A2A live real-time examples — streaming arena replay + conversational artifact feed (B7 intake decomposition)
_meta.author: a2a-live-designer (design seat)
_meta.grounding: Kim's verbatim ask: 'can we make the A2A examples live so they occur in real time user initiated?'. Two shipped surfaces to make real-time: (1) the arena page's dev-only live seam (site/pages/a2a-tic-tac-toe.ts wireLiveOverlay + site/lib/arena-live-transport.ts) is BATCH — the dev proxy (a2a/tools/arena/dev-proxy-plugin.ts) already streams NDJSON per transcript event server-side (match.ts onEvent, 'review finding 4 closed'), but the client reads res.text() whole; (2) the artifact-feed page (site/pages/a2a-artifact-feed.ts) reserves a named live-arm seam (bridge LLD §7: probeLive + DEV dynamic import) and annotates interactions 'Composed locally — not sent'. Reuse substrate: produce() (a2ui/tools/agent/produce.ts — the bounded generate→validate→self-correct loop, validate-then-stream), the bridge builders (a2ui/tools/pipeline/transports/a2a.ts — wrapClientTurn/wrapServerTurn/unwrapTurn, HV-8 shapes), the providers.json registry + ADR-0073 trust boundary, live-proxy-transport.ts's incremental NDJSON line reader (the idiom to extract), loadFeed/deriveEntry (site/lib/artifact-feed.ts), loadTranscript/buildReplaySteps (site/lib/arena-replay.ts). Constraints held, not relitigated: C4 recorded-default (live = dev-only behind the server-side-key proxy), SPEC-N1 zero-dep a2a src/, SPEC-N2 key/network hygiene, the isolation gate must accept completed live transcripts via the SAME checker, generator≠critic + deterministic gates for everything new.
_meta.note: Both planes derived independently: OUTSIDE-IN from the two demo surfaces' structural grain (shared stream plumbing → arena slice → feed slice → gates → doc re-sync), INSIDE-OUT from the user-visible verb set (watch moves land live, cancel, verdict-over-completed, type-and-send over A2A, watch prose+artifacts render, interact→next turn, degrade on fault) plus the standing doctrine (same-checker, fail-closed logs, static-build hygiene, independent review, context-is-memory). Design records: ADR-0116 (proposed — NOT self-ratified), spec/a2a-foundations.spec.md v0.6 (SPEC-R17/R18 widening), lld/a2a-live-realtime.lld.md (one LLD for both slices — the corpus-docs B4/B5 one-doc precedent). ADR number 0116 verified free (README tail = 0115; 0108 is a known hole, never reused).

nodes.label: A2A live real-time examples (B7)
shared streaming plumbing
NDJSON line-reader helper (site/lib/ndjson-lines.ts — extracted from live-proxy-transport.ts's inline reader; that module refactored onto it)
nodes.accept: one exported async-iterable line reader over a ReadableStream (decode, buffer-split on \n, trimmed non-empty lines, tail flush); live-proxy-transport.ts imports it with byte-identical yield behavior (its existing consumers stay green); unit test covers chunk boundaries splitting a line mid-byte and a no-trailing-newline tail

nodes.label: arena real-time (slice A)

incremental replay accumulator (site/lib/arena-replay.ts — createReplayAccumulator: consume one parsed transcript line at a time, maintain the board, emit appended ReplayStep(s) + per-seat context lines)
nodes.accept: chunk-equivalence gate green: for EACH committed matches/*.jsonl fixture, feeding lines one-at-a-time (and again split at adversarial chunk boundaries through the n1a reader) yields steps deep-equal to buildReplaySteps(loadTranscript(raw)) and context lines equal to buildIsolationReport(t).contexts, with isComplete() true after the final line; a malformed/out-of-order line makes the accumulator report a fault (fail-closed), never throw — with a firing negative control; a fixture with its trailing game-end event dropped leaves isComplete() FALSE while its prefix still VALIDATES (validateTranscript has no terminal-event requirement — completion is the accumulator's tracked fact, the doc-review HIGH)

nodes.label: streaming live-match client (site/lib/arena-live-transport.ts — runLiveMatchStream: POST + incremental line yield via n1a + AbortSignal; accumulates the raw text verbatim)
nodes.accept: the function yields each NDJSON line as it arrives and exposes the accumulated raw text on completion (byte-identical to what runLiveMatch would have returned — asserted against a stubbed streamed response); an abort mid-stream terminates the fetch and surfaces a typed cancelled outcome, not a throw-through

nodes.label: runner abort seam (a2a/tools/arena/match.ts RunMatchOptions.signal + dev-proxy-plugin.ts wiring: client disconnect/cancel → AbortController → the runner stops between/within moves; partial transcript discarded)
nodes.accept: runMatch with an aborted signal stops without emitting further events, closes all four channel endpoints (the existing finally), and rejects/returns a typed aborted outcome; the proxy aborts on the response's close event; a scripted-match test proves no event is emitted after abort and teardown state is clean; the scripted CI transcript path is byte-identical when no signal is passed (zero-regression control)

nodes.label: arena page live mode (site/pages/a2a-tic-tac-toe.ts — append steps as events arrive, follow-tail scrubber that never yanks a user who scrubbed back, verdict panel in 'pending — runs over the completed transcript' state, a Cancel control, completed stream → the EXISTING loadTranscript/applyLoaded('live') path unchanged)
nodes.accept: under vite dev with a key: moves render into the replay UI as they are played (manually verified) with the scrubber usable during and after; the page's done state gates on the accumulator's isComplete() — NEVER on stream end alone (a clean res.end() of a partial validates as a prefix; the completion gate is what refuses it) — and only then derives replay + isolation verdict from the accumulated text through the SAME loadTranscript path the batch fixtures use (code-identical call site); Cancel aborts and returns to the previously selected recorded fixture; a stream ending incomplete shows the error panel and leaves every recorded fixture working; the static build carries none of this (DEV dynamic import unchanged)

nodes.label: feed live arm (slice B)

part-frame protocol (a2a/tools/feed/frames.ts — pure, browser-safe: header frame {messageId, contextId, taskId?, role, parts: count} + one frame per A2aPart, all derived from ONE wrapServerTurn-built message; the declared part count is the COMPLETION INVARIANT — truncation is decidable from the frames, never inferred from stream end; reassembly back to the identical A2aMessage)
nodes.accept: round-trip gate green: framesOf(wrapServerTurn(msgs, opts)) reassembled deep-equals the original message for prose-only, artifact-only, mixed, and zero-part turns (parts:0 with no part frames = a valid empty turn, not a fault); negative controls fire: a part frame before the header, complete() with fewer or more parts than the header declares (the truncation/overrun fault the count makes decidable), and a foreign frame kind each yield a typed fault, never a throw

nodes.label: feed-session derivation (a2a/tools/feed/feed-session.ts — pure: an ordered A2A message log → the produce() Session {turns} + the current TurnInput; user TextParts → intent text, a tagged client DataPart → the framed client message, agent turns → their envelopes re-serialized as the stored assistant JSONL)
nodes.accept: deterministic unit tests green: the committed artifact-feed fixture's log derives a Session whose assistant turns contain exactly the fixture's unwrapped envelope JSONL and whose user turns match the TextPart text; a log whose last message is not role:'user' or lacks the HV-8 caps key is a typed rejection; derivation is a pure function of the log (same input ⇒ deep-equal output, asserted twice)

nodes.label: feed dev proxy (a2a/tools/feed/dev-proxy-plugin.ts — mount /__a2a/feed, apply:'serve': GET /status boolean+count; POST {feed: lines, provider, model} → resolvePair allowlist → validateA2a every line at pin 0.3.0 → caps-presence rejection (400, coded) → sessionFromFeed → produce() with the a2ui deps → ONE wrapServerTurn message → stream its frames; stateless — no server-held conversation)
nodes.accept: with a stub provider injected at the seam the proxy is testable offline: a valid log streams header+part frames whose reassembly validates (validateA2a clean, caps rule holds on every inbound); a caps-less last message and an invalid line are each rejected 400 with a coded error body (firing controls); no key value ever appears in any response body; apply:'serve' keeps it out of vite build

nodes.label: feed entry derivation refactor (site/lib/artifact-feed.ts — export the per-message deriveFeedEntry loadFeed already computes internally, so the live arm renders through the SAME derivation, no fork)
nodes.accept: loadFeed's behavior is byte-identical after the refactor (existing tests green unchanged); deriveFeedEntry(message, index) returns the same FeedEntry loadFeed produces for that line — asserted for every entry of the committed fixture

nodes.label: feed live transport (site/lib/feed-live-transport.ts — DEV-only dynamic-import module: probeFeedLive() + sendTurn(feedLines, selection, signal) yielding frames via n1a and returning the reassembled agent A2aMessage)
nodes.accept: mirrors the probeLive()/fetch shape (no VITE_* reference, no key); sendTurn yields part frames incrementally and returns a reassembled message deep-equal to the n3a reassembly of the same frame stream (one reassembler, imported — not a second implementation); non-2xx surfaces the proxy's coded error text

nodes.label: feed page live overlay (site/pages/a2a-artifact-feed.ts — the reserved LLD §7 seam filled: probe-gated 'Go live' + composer (dev-only, dynamic import); fresh live conversation with a synthesized header line + contextId; user turns wrapClientTurn-built client-side and appended to the client-held log; agent frames render progressively (prose → bubble text, each DataPart → host.ingest); the reassembled message joins the log ONLY on turn completion; verdict recomputed per turn via loadFeed over the accumulated log; artifact actions genuinely sent as the next turn; faulted turns annotated and excluded from the log; Reset returns to the recorded fixture)
nodes.accept: under vite dev with a key: typing a message renders a user bubble whose capabilities disclosure shows the REAL wrapClientTurn wire, the agent's prose + artifacts render as frames arrive, clicking an artifact control dispatches a genuine next turn (the 'Composed locally — not sent' annotation is absent in live mode), and the verdict line stays derived from loadFeed over the accumulated log after every turn; a ProduceHalt/fault turn shows an annotation, never joins the log, and the composer re-enables; Reset restores the recorded fixture exactly; the recorded default path is byte-identical when the overlay never activates

nodes.label: gates & hygiene
static-build hygiene proof (vite build + dist grep for both new mounts and any key/env name; every live module reached only via DEV-guarded dynamic import)
nodes.accept: vite build succeeds and grep of dist/ for /__a2a/feed, /__a2a/arena, and provider env-key names is clean (the SPEC-N2 manual proof, recorded in the wave handoff)

nodes.label: same-checker proof over live artifacts (offline structural tests: a stub-provider live-feed conversation log passes the SAME loadFeed checks the fixture gate runs; a completed streamed arena run's accumulated text passes loadTranscript + checkIsolation — the same checker, no fork)
nodes.accept: npm test carries both legs offline (stub provider / committed fixtures replayed through the streaming path): the accumulated artifacts pass the shared checkers; the arena truncation negative shows the prefix VALIDATES but isComplete() refuses it (gate bites, validator honestly doesn't), and the feed negative forces a raw frame line into a log and loadFeed FAILS it — each fail-closed control fires at the layer that owns refusal

nodes.label: independent review pass (generator ≠ critic: code review of both slices against this LLD before the wave commit)
nodes.accept: the reviewer seat records GO with zero blockers before the commit; a NO-GO loops back to the owning slice

nodes.label: doc re-sync (serial: bridge LLD §7 live-arm seam row flipped to realized-by, arena page/file banner notes re-synced (the 'nothing mid-flight to render yet' rationale expires), SPEC §7 trace + PRD §6 B7 rows, ADR README row, CHANGELOG)
nodes.accept: no owning doc still describes the live seam as batch-only or 'structure only'; trace/harness doc gates pass; the re-sync lands in the same change that invalidates (context-is-memory)

actions.label: watch a live match render move-by-move as it is played
scrub the replay during and after a live match (follow-tail; never yank a user who scrubbed back)
cancel a live match mid-flight (user-initiated; server work aborted)
derive the isolation verdict from a completed live transcript via the SAME checker
degrade cleanly on a mid-match stream fault (recorded default unharmed)
type a message that genuinely travels as an A2A Message (real caps handshake, server-verified)
receive an agent turn as prose + live A2UI artifacts, rendering as parts arrive
interact with a live artifact and have it drive the next conversational turn
hold the conversation client-side (stateless proxy; the A2A log is the session substrate)
recompute the feed verdict per turn with the same standing checks
exclude faulted/partial turns from the canonical log (fail-closed)
read NDJSON streams incrementally through one shared idiom
keep the static build free of any live mount, key, or proxy path
prove streamed derivation ≡ batch derivation (chunk-boundary equivalence)
prove frame reassembly ≡ the one-construction A2A message (round-trip + biting negatives)
reject a caps-less or schema-invalid inbound message with a coded error
abort the match runner cleanly when the client disconnects
start a fresh live conversation and reset back to the recorded fixture
derive the model session deterministically from the A2A message log
pass independent review before the wave commit (generator ≠ critic)
keep every owning doc true in the same change (context-is-memory)

edges.why: the streaming match client reads its response through the shared line reader
the feed live transport reads its frame stream through the same reader
the page's live-append rendering consumes the accumulator's appended steps
the page drives the streaming client and wires its cancel signal
Cancel is only honest if the server actually stops — the page control depends on the runner seam
the proxy emits frames the protocol module defines (one construction)
the browser reassembles through the SAME module, never a second implementation
the proxy derives the produce() session from the posted log via this pure function
the transport speaks the proxy's mount + frame contract
live bubbles render through the same derivation the recorded feed uses
the page overlay drives turns through the live transport
hygiene is proven over the finished pages
hygiene is proven over the finished pages
the arena same-checker leg replays fixtures through the streaming derivation
the feed same-checker leg runs a stub-provider conversation through the proxy path
review grades the finished, hygiene-proven slices
review grades the finished, gate-proven slices
docs flip to 'realized' only after the reviewed wave is real
